translations.literal: Grand counsellor of the palace Gu Yong submitted a memorial pleading for Tang, saying: Your servant has heard that Chu had Ziyu Dechen, and Duke Wen for him sat with empty mat;
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong, grand counsellor of the palace, pleaded for Chen Tang: When Chu had Dechen, Duke Wen of Jin rose from his mat in respect—

sentences.zh: 趙有廉頗、馬服，強秦不敢窺兵井陘；

translations.literal: Zhao had Lian Po and Lord Ma, and mighty Qin did not dare spy troops at Jingxing;
translations.idiomatic: when Zhao had Lian Po and Lord Ma, even Qin dared not probe the Jingxing pass;

sentences.zh: 近漢有郅都、魏尚，匈奴不敢南鄉沙幕。

translations.literal: In recent Han there were Zhi Du and Wei Shang, and the Xiongnu did not dare turn south toward the sandy desert.
translations.idiomatic: in our own Han, Zhi Du and Wei Shang kept the Xiongnu from turning south toward the desert.

sentences.zh: 由是言之，戰克之將，國之爪牙，不可不重也。

translations.literal: From this speaking, generals who conquer in battle are the state's talons and fangs; they cannot but be valued.
translations.idiomatic: Victorious generals are the fangs and claws of the state and must be honored.

sentences.zh: 蓋『君子聞鼓鼙之聲，則思將率之臣』。

translations.literal: It is said: 'When the gentleman hears drum and little drum, he thinks of the general and leader ministers.'
translations.idiomatic: The classic says, At the sound of war drums the gentleman remembers his field commanders.

sentences.zh: 竊見關內侯陳湯，前使副西域都護，忿郅支之無道，閔王誅之不加，策慮□億，義勇奮發，卒興師奔逝，橫厲烏孫，逾集都賴，屠三重城，斬郅支首，報十年之逋誅，雪邊吏之宿恥，威震百蠻，武暢西海，漢元以來，征伐方外之將，未嘗有也。

translations.literal: Your servant has seen Marquis within the passes Chen Tang, formerly sent as deputy to the Western Regions protector-general, resenting Zhizhi's lack of way, grieving the king did not punish him, stratagems and plans in the hundred millions, righteous courage roused, finally raising troops and rushing afar, crossing and cleaving Wusun, gathering beyond Talas, slaughtering threefold walls, beheading Zhizhi's head, repaying ten years' deferred execution, wiping border clerks' long-standing shame, awe shaking the hundred barbarians, martial might flowing to the western sea—since Han Yuan, generals who campaigned beyond the borders, there has never been his like.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Chen Tang, as deputy to the protector-general, burned with rage at Zhizhi's cruelty and grief that the throne had not punished him; he laid immeasurable plans, roused loyal courage, marched across Wusun, closed on the Talas, stormed three rings of walls, and struck off Zhizhi's head—settling a ten-year debt of blood, wiping away the frontier's shame, awing every tribe and carrying Han arms to the western sea. Since Emperor Yuan's reign no frontier general has matched him.

sentences.zh: 今湯坐言事非是，幽囚久系，歷時不決，執憲之吏欲致之大辟。

translations.literal: Now Tang sits crime for speaking affairs wrongly, is darkly imprisoned long bound, passes time undecided; clerks who hold the statutes wish to bring him to great execution.
translations.idiomatic: Now he languishes in chains for a mistaken memorial while petty judges press for his execution.

sentences.zh: 昔白起為秦將，南拔郢都，北坑趙括，以纖介之過，賜死杜郵，秦民憐之，莫不隕涕。

translations.literal: Formerly Bai Qi was Qin general, south took Ying capital, north buried Zhao Kuo alive; for a hair's-breadth fault he was ordered to die at Duyou; Qin people pitied him, none did not shed tears.
translations.idiomatic: Bai Qi stormed Ying for Qin and buried Zhao Kuo's army at Changping, yet for a trifle he was forced to kill himself at Duyou while all Qin wept for him.

sentences.zh: 今湯親秉鉞，席捲喋血萬里之外，薦功祖廟，告類上帝，介冑之士靡不慕義。

translations.literal: Now Tang personally grasped the battle-axe, rolled up like mat and blood-smear beyond ten thousand li, presented merit at the ancestral temple, reported category to High God; armored men none did not admire righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang himself bore the axe and shed blood ten thousand li from home, hung his trophies in the ancestral temple, and reported to Heaven—every soldier admires his honor.

sentences.zh: 以言事為罪，無赫赫之惡。

translations.literal: Taking speech on affairs as crime—there is no glaring evil.
translations.idiomatic: His offense was words, not some glaring crime.

sentences.zh: 《周書》曰：『記人之功，忘人之過，宜為君者也。』

translations.literal: The Zhou Documents says: 'Record people's merit, forget people's faults—this is fitting for one who would be ruler.'
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou Documents says, Remember merit and forget small faults—that is how a ruler acts.

sentences.zh: 夫犬馬有勞於人，尚加帷蓋之報，況國之功臣者哉！

translations.literal: If dogs and horses have toil for men, still they are given curtain-and-cover return; how much more the state's meritorious ministers!
translations.idiomatic: Even a dog or horse that serves receives a decent burial—how much more a pillar of the state!

sentences.zh: 竊恐陛下忽於鼙鼓之聲，不察《周書》之意，而忘帷蓋之施，庸臣遇湯，卒從吏議，使百姓介然有秦民之恨，非所以厲死難之臣也。」

translations.literal: Your servant fears Your Majesty will neglect the sound of little drums, not examine the Zhou Documents' meaning, and forget curtain-and-cover kindness; mediocre ministers will treat Tang and in the end follow clerks' deliberations, causing the hundred surnames distinctly to have Qin people's resentment—not the means to encourage those who die in hardship for the state.
translations.idiomatic: I fear Your Majesty may heed petty clerks, forget the Zhou Documents' teaching and the kindness owed a war-horse, let mediocrities decide Chen Tang's fate, and leave the people with the same bitterness they felt for Qin—hardly the way to hearten men who risk death for the throne.

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子出湯，奪爵為士伍。

translations.literal: When the memorial was submitted, the Son of Heaven released Tang, stripped rank making him common soldier.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor freed him from prison but stripped his rank to commoner status.

sentences.zh: 後數歲，西域都護段會宗為烏孫兵所圍，驛騎上書，願發城郭敦煌兵以自救。

translations.literal: After several years, Western Regions Protector-general Duan Huizong was surrounded by Wusun troops; relay riders submitted a memorial wishing to mobilize walled states and Dunhuang troops to save himself.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later Protector-general Duan Huizong was besieged by Wusun; he sent riders to ask for relief from the oasis militias and Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 丞相王商、大將軍王鳳及百僚議數日不決。

translations.literal: Chancellor Wang Shang, Grand General Wang Feng, and the hundred officials deliberated several days without decision.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Wang Shang, Grand General Wang Feng, and the whole court debated for days without agreement.

sentences.zh: 鳳言：「湯多籌策，習外國事，可問。」

translations.literal: Feng said: Tang has many stratagems, is practiced in foreign affairs—he can be asked.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng said Chen Tang knew frontier strategy and should be consulted.

sentences.zh: 上召湯見宣室。

translations.literal: The emperor summoned Tang to audience in the Bright Chamber.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned him to the Bright Chamber.

sentences.zh: 湯擊郅支時中塞病，兩臂不詘申。

translations.literal: When Tang struck Zhizhi he contracted frontier illness; his two arms could not flex and extend.
translations.idiomatic: Campaigning against Zhizhi he had contracted a frontier ailment that left his arms stiff.

sentences.zh: 湯入見，有詔毋拜，示以會宗奏。

translations.literal: When Tang entered audience there was an edict not to bow; he was shown Huizong's memorial.
translations.idiomatic: He was ordered not to bow and was shown Duan Huizong's plea.

sentences.zh: 湯辭謝，曰：「將相九卿皆賢材通明，小臣罷癃，不足以策大事。」

translations.literal: Tang declined, saying: Generals, ministers, and nine ministers are all worthy talents clear and bright; your petty servant is worn and crippled, insufficient to plan great affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang demurred: The generals, ministers, and nine ministers are wise men; your servant is a broken old soldier unfit to counsel on great matters.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「國家有急，君其毋讓。」

translations.literal: The emperor said: The state has emergency; sir, do not decline.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor said, The state is in crisis—do not refuse.

sentences.zh: 對曰：「臣以為此必無可憂也。」

translations.literal: He replied: Your servant thinks in this there surely is nothing to worry about.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang answered, There is nothing to worry about.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「何以言之？」

translations.literal: The emperor said: By what do you say this?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked how he knew.

sentences.zh: 湯曰：「夫胡兵五而當漢兵一，何者？

translations.literal: Tang said: Hu troops five match one Han soldier—why?
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang said, It takes five Hu to match one Han soldier—why?

sentences.zh: 兵刃樸鈍，弓弩不利。

translations.literal: Weapons are plain and dull; crossbows are not sharp.
translations.idiomatic: Their blades are soft iron and their bows weak.

sentences.zh: 今聞頗得漢巧，然猶三而當一。

translations.literal: Now I hear they have somewhat obtained Han skill, yet still three match one.
translations.idiomatic: They have picked up some Han craft, but three of them still equal one of ours.

sentences.zh: 又兵法曰『客倍而主人半然後敵』，今圍會宗者人眾不足以勝會宗，唯陛下勿憂！

translations.literal: Moreover military methods says 'guest double and host half then they match'; now those besieging Huizong in numbers are insufficient to overcome Huizong—only Your Majesty do not worry!
translations.idiomatic: The canon says the attacker needs twice the defender's numbers; the besiegers lack the strength to overcome Duan Huizong—Your Majesty need not fret.

sentences.zh: 且兵輕行五十里，重行三十里，今會宗欲發城郭敦煌，歷時乃至，所謂報仇之兵，非救急之用也！」

translations.literal: Moreover troops lightly march fifty li a day, heavily thirty li a day; now Huizong wishes to mobilize walled states and Dunhuang—after time passes they arrive—what is called vengeance troops, not urgent relief's use!
translations.idiomatic: Light troops make fifty li a day, heavy baggage thirty; relief from Dunhuang would arrive too late for a rescue—it would be vengeance, not succor.

sentences.zh: 上曰：「奈何？

translations.literal: The emperor said: What is to be done?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked what could be done.

sentences.zh: 其解可必乎？

translations.literal: Can the relief be certain?
translations.idiomatic: Can the siege be lifted for certain?

sentences.zh: 度何時解？」

translations.literal: Estimate when it will be relieved?
translations.idiomatic: When will it end?

sentences.zh: 湯知烏孫瓦合，不能久攻，故事不過數日。

translations.literal: Tang knew Wusun was tile-mortar joined, unable long to attack; therefore the affair would not pass several days.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang knew the Wusun host was a brittle coalition that could not sustain a long siege; the matter would end within days.

sentences.zh: 因對曰：「已解矣！」

translations.literal: Thereupon he replied: It is already relieved!
translations.idiomatic: He answered, It is already over.

sentences.zh: 詘指計其日，曰：「不出五日，當有吉語聞。」

translations.literal: He bent fingers counting the days, saying: Within five days there ought to be good news heard.
translations.idiomatic: He counted on his fingers and said, Within five days good news will arrive.

sentences.zh: 居四日，軍書到，言已解。

translations.literal: After four days, military dispatch arrived, saying already relieved.
translations.idiomatic: Four days later dispatches arrived reporting the siege lifted.

sentences.zh: 大將軍鳳奏以為從事中郎，莫府事一決於湯。

translations.literal: Grand General Feng memorialized appointing him attendant gentleman in the bureau; headquarters affairs were all decided by Tang.
translations.idiomatic: Grand General Wang Feng recommended him as attendant in his bureau and left headquarters business to his judgment.

sentences.zh: 湯明法令，善因事為勢，納說多從。

translations.literal: Tang was clear on statutes and orders, good at riding affairs to make momentum; submitted persuasions were mostly followed.
translations.idiomatic: He knew the law, shaped policy to circumstance, and his advice was usually taken.

sentences.zh: 常受人金錢作章奏，卒以此敗。

translations.literal: He constantly received people's gold and cash to draft memorials; in the end by this he was ruined.
translations.idiomatic: He habitually took bribes to draft memorials, and that was his ruin in the end.

sentences.zh: 初，湯與將作大匠解萬年相善。

translations.literal: At first Tang and Superintendent of Construction Xie Wannian were on good terms.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang had long been friendly with Superintendent of Construction Xie Wannian.

sentences.zh: 自元帝時，渭陵不復徙民起邑。

translations.literal: From Emperor Yuan's time, Weiling no longer moved people to raise towns.
translations.idiomatic: Since Emperor Yuan's reign the court had stopped transplanting commoners to build new towns around Weiling.

sentences.zh: 成帝起初陵，數年後，樂霸陵曲亭南，更營之。

translations.literal: Emperor Cheng began the first tomb; after several years he delighted in south of Baling's Queting and changed to build there.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng began his first mausoleum site, but after a few years he preferred the ground south of Queting near Baling and moved the project there.

sentences.zh: 萬年與湯議，以為：「武帝時工楊光以所作數可意，自致將作大匠，及大司農、中丞耿壽昌造杜陵賜爵關內侯，將作大匠乘馬延年以勞苦秩中二千石；

translations.literal: Wannian discussed with Tang, thinking: In Emperor Wu's time craftsman Yang Guang because his constructions repeatedly pleased was himself promoted to superintendent of construction; and Grand Minister of Agriculture Vice Censor Geng Shouchang built Duling and was granted marquis within the passes; Superintendent of Construction Chengma Yannian by toil and hardship ranked at two thousand piculs;
translations.idiomatic: Xie Wannian said to Chen Tang: Under Emperor Wu the builder Yang Guang rose to superintendent of construction because the emperor liked his work; Geng Shouchang of the ministry of finance built Duling and won a marquisate within the passes; Chengma Yannian as superintendent earned two thousand piculs for his labors—

sentences.zh: 今作初陵而營起邑居，成大功，萬年亦當蒙重賞。

translations.literal: Now building the first tomb and raising town dwellings completes great merit; Wannian too ought to receive heavy reward.
translations.idiomatic: if we now build the first tomb and found a settlement, the achievement will be great and Wannian should share a rich reward.

sentences.zh: 子公妻家在長安，兒子生長長安，不樂東方，宜求徙，可得賜田宅，俱善。」

translations.literal: Zi Gong's (Chen Tang's) wife's family is in Chang'an; sons were born and grew in Chang'an; they do not delight in the east; it is fitting to seek removal; can obtain granted fields and houses—both are good.
translations.idiomatic: Your wife's people are in Chang'an and your boys grew up there—they dread moving east. If you petition to relocate with the project you will win grants of land and houses. Everyone profits.

sentences.zh: 湯心利之，即上封事言：「初陵，京師之地，最為肥美，可立一縣。

translations.literal: Tang's heart profited by it; immediately he submitted a sealed memorial saying: The first tomb is capital region ground, most fat and beautiful; a county can be established.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang saw the profit and memorialized that the new tomb district lay on the richest soil near the capital and should be organized as a county.

sentences.zh: 天下民不徙諸陵三十餘歲矣，關東富人益眾，多規良田，役使貧民，可徙初陵，以強京師，衰弱諸侯，又使中家以下得均貧富，湯願與妻子家屬徙初陵，為天下先。」

translations.literal: All under heaven's people have not been moved to the various tombs for over thirty years; east of the pass the rich grow more numerous, mostly plotting good fields, conscripting and using the poor; they can be moved to the first tomb to strengthen the capital, weaken feudal lords, and also cause middle households down to equal rich and poor; Tang wishes with wife children and family dependents to move to the first tomb, for all under heaven taking the lead.
translations.idiomatic: For thirty years no mass transplant to imperial tombs has been ordered; the rich east of the pass monopolize good land and exploit the poor. Moving them to the new site would strengthen the capital, clip the great houses, and level wealth among the middling and poor. Your servant asks to move there with his household and lead the empire by example.

sentences.zh: 於是天子從其計，果起昌陵邑，後徙內郡國民。

translations.literal: Thereupon the Son of Heaven followed his plan; indeed they raised Changling town, afterward moving inner commandery and kingdom people.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor accepted the plan, founded the town of Changling, and later moved tens of thousands from the interior commanderies.

sentences.zh: 萬年自詭三年可成，後卒不就，群臣多言其不便者。

translations.literal: Wannian himself promised three years could finish it; afterward in the end it was not completed; many ministers spoke of its inconvenience.
translations.idiomatic: Xie Wannian had sworn to finish in three years but never delivered, and a host of officials attacked the project as ruinous.

sentences.zh: 下有司議，皆曰：「昌陵因卑為高，積土為山，度便房猶在平地上，客土之中不保幽冥之靈，淺外不固，卒徒工庸以巨萬數，至然脂火夜作，取土東山，且與谷同賈。

translations.literal: It was sent down to offices for deliberation; all said: Changling raised low to high, piled earth to make mountains; measuring the side chambers still on level ground, within imported earth does not secure the dark realm's spirits; shallow outside not firm; conscript laborers and craftsmen by myriads of ten thousands; reaching burning fat lamps night work; taking earth from eastern hills—and moreover at the same price as grain.
translations.idiomatic: Officials reported in unison: Changling piles fill on low ground to fake a hill; the burial chambers still sit on the original flat soil under a heap of imported earth that cannot shelter the imperial shade; the work is shallow and unstable; labor runs into tens of thousands of man-years; crews burn oil lamps through the night; earth hauled from the eastern hills costs as much as grain.

sentences.zh: 作治數年，天下遍被其勞，國家罷敝，府臧空虛，下至眾庶，熬熬苦之。

translations.literal: Construction for several years, all under heaven everywhere suffered its toil; the state was worn out, treasuries were empty; down to the masses, bitterly they suffered.
translations.idiomatic: Years of building have exhausted the realm, emptied the treasury, and left the common people groaning under the levy.

sentences.zh: 故陵因天性，據真土，處勢高敞，旁近祖考，前又已有十年功緒，宜還復故陵，勿徙民。」

translations.literal: The former tomb relies on heaven's nature, occupies true earth, sits in a high and open situation, near neighboring ancestors; before there was already ten years' work progress; it is fitting to return to the former tomb and not move the people.
translations.idiomatic: The old site follows natural contours on native soil, lies high and open beside earlier imperial graves, and already has ten years of sound work; the court should return to it and stop moving people.

sentences.zh: 上乃下詔罷昌陵，語在《成紀》。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon issued an edict abolishing Changling; the words are in the 'Annals of Cheng.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then canceled Changling, as told in the Annals of Emperor Cheng.

sentences.zh: 丞相、御史請廢昌陵邑中室，奏未下，人以問湯：「第宅不徹，得毋復發徙？」

translations.literal: The chancellor and imperial counselor requested abolishing Changling town center chambers; the memorial had not yet been issued when people asked Tang: If houses are not torn down, could it not again issue relocation?
translations.idiomatic: While the chancellor and imperial counselor's memorial to raze the houses at Changling still awaited approval, people asked Chen Tang whether failure to demolish the mansions meant another forced migration was coming.

sentences.zh: 湯曰：「縣官且順聽群臣言，猶且復發徙之也。」

translations.literal: Tang said: The court for now will comply and listen to ministers' words, yet still will again issue relocation.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang said, The court may bow to the ministers' advice for the moment, but it will still order people moved again.

sentences.zh: 時，成都侯商新為大司馬衛將軍輔政，素不善湯。

translations.literal: At the time Marquis of Chengdu Shang had newly become grand marshal and guard general assisting government and had never been on good terms with Tang.
translations.idiomatic: Marquis Wang Shang of Chengdu had just become regent as grand marshal and guard general and had long disliked Chen Tang.

sentences.zh: 商聞此語，白湯惑眾，下獄治，按驗諸所犯。

translations.literal: When Shang heard these words he reported Tang for deluding the masses, cast him into prison for trial, and investigated all offenses.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang reported him for inciting the crowd, had him jailed, and investigated every charge.

sentences.zh: 湯前為騎都尉王莽上書言：「父早死，獨不封，母明君共養皇太后，尤勞苦，宜封。」

translations.literal: Tang formerly as cavalry colonel submitted a memorial for Wang Mang saying: Father died early, alone was not enfeoffed; mother Mingjun together nourished the empress dowager, especially toiled; she ought to be enfeoffed.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang had once, as cavalry colonel, memorialized on behalf of Wang Mang that Mang's late father deserved a posthumous fief and his mother, who had nursed the empress dowager, deserved honor.

sentences.zh: 竟為新都侯。

translations.literal: In the end he became marquis of Xindu.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang was eventually enfeoffed as Marquis of Xindu.

sentences.zh: 後皇太后同母弟苟參為水衡都尉，死，子人及為侍中，參妻欲為人及求封，湯受其金五十斤，許為求比上奏。

translations.literal: Later the empress dowager's uterine younger brother Gou Can was superintendent of waters and parks; he died; son Renji became palace attendant; Can's wife wished to seek enfeoffment for Renji; Tang received her fifty jin of gold and promised to seek precedent and submit memorial.
translations.idiomatic: When the empress dowager's half-brother Gou Can died, his son Renji became a palace attendant; Gou's widow paid Chen Tang fifty jin of gold to memorialize for a marquisate on the same pattern.

sentences.zh: 弘農太守張匡坐臧百萬以上，狡猾不道，有詔即訊，恐下獄，使人報湯。

translations.literal: Hongnong grand administrator Zhang Kuang sat embezzlement over a million, crafty and immoral in conduct; there was an edict for immediate interrogation; fearing entering prison, he sent a man to report to Tang.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Administrator Zhang Kuang of Hongnong, charged with embezzling more than a million cash, sent an agent to Chen Tang when an edict ordered his immediate questioning.

sentences.zh: 湯為訟罪，得逾冬月，許射錢二百萬，皆此類也。

translations.literal: Tang for him pled guilt, obtaining passage beyond winter month, promised two million cash—all was of this kind.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang pled his case so the trial slipped past winter and took two million cash in bribes—his habits were all of this sort.

sentences.zh: 事在赦前。

translations.literal: The affair was before the amnesty.
translations.idiomatic: These acts predated the amnesty.

sentences.zh: 後東萊郡黑龍冬出，人以問湯，湯曰：「是所謂玄門開。

translations.literal: Later in Donglai commandery a black dragon appeared in winter; people asked Tang; Tang said: This is what is called the Dark Gate opening.
translations.idiomatic: When a black dragon appeared in winter in Donglai, people asked Chen Tang; he said the Dark Gate of Heaven had opened.

sentences.zh: 微行數出，出入不時，故龍以非時出也。」

translations.literal: Disguised outings repeatedly go out, entering and exiting not at proper times; therefore the dragon appears out of season.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's incognito outings come at odd hours, so the dragon appears out of season.

sentences.zh: 又言當復發徙，傳相語者十餘人。

translations.literal: Again he said relocation would again be issued; those who passed word to each other were over ten persons.
translations.idiomatic: He also said another migration was coming; more than ten people repeated the rumor.

sentences.zh: 丞相御史奏：「湯惑眾不道，妄稱詐歸異於上，非所宜言，大不敬。」

translations.literal: The chancellor and imperial counselor memorialized: Tang deluded the masses and was immoral in conduct, falsely called strange prodigies differing from above; not what ought to be spoken—great disrespect.
translations.idiomatic: The chancellor and imperial counselor charged him with misleading the people, inventing omens that contradicted the throne, and gross disrespect.

sentences.zh: 廷尉增壽議，以為：「不道無正法，以所犯劇易為罪，臣下承用失其中，故移獄廷尉，無比者先以聞，所以正刑罰，重人命也。

translations.literal: Commandant of justice Zengshou deliberated, thinking: 'Immoral conduct' has no fixed law; by the offense's severity or ease one fixes guilt; ministers receiving application miss the mean; therefore shift the case to the commandant of justice; when there is no precedent first report it—this is how to correct punishments and value human life.
translations.idiomatic: The commandant of justice ruled that the vague charge of immoral conduct must be graded by harm done; doubtful cases belong with his office and should be reported before precedent is set, to keep justice fair and spare lives.

sentences.zh: 明主哀憫百姓，下制書罷昌陵勿徙吏民，已申布。

translations.literal: The clear ruler pitied the hundred surnames, issued an edict abolishing Changling and not moving clerks and people; it has already been proclaimed.
translations.idiomatic: The wise emperor pitied the people, canceled Changling, and forbade further removals; the edict has been published.

sentences.zh: 湯妄以意相謂且復發徙，雖頗驚動，所流行者少，百姓不為變，不可謂惑眾。

translations.literal: Tang recklessly by intent told each other that relocation would again be issued; although quite startling, those through whom it spread were few; the hundred surnames did not change—cannot be called deluding the masses.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang guessed aloud that another migration was coming; it caused some stir among a handful of gossips but did not move the populace and is not sedition.

sentences.zh: 湯稱詐，虛設不然之事，非所宜言，大不敬也。」

translations.literal: Tang called false things, hollowly set up what is not so—not what ought to be spoken; it is great disrespect.
translations.idiomatic: He invented false omens and spoke what no subject may say toward the throne—that is grave disrespect.

sentences.zh: 制曰：「廷尉增壽當是。

translations.literal: Imperial rescript said: Commandant of justice Zengshou's judgment is correct.
translations.idiomatic: The rescript read: The commandant of justice is right.

sentences.zh: 湯前有討郅支單于功，其免湯為庶人，徙邊。」

translations.literal: Tang before had merit in punishing Chanyu Zhizhi; let him be spared to commoner status and banished to the border.
translations.idiomatic: Because he once destroyed Zhizhi, spare his life, strip him to commoner rank, and exile him to the frontier.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「故將作大匠萬年佞邪不忠，妄為巧詐，多賦斂，煩繇役，興卒暴之作，卒徒蒙辜，死者連屬，毒流眾庶，海內怨望。

translations.literal: It also said: Former Superintendent of Construction Wannian was sycophantic and evil, not loyal, recklessly practiced craft and deceit, many taxes and levies, vexing corvée labor, raised sudden conscript works; conscripts bore guilt, the dead linked in series; poison flowed to the masses; within the seas resentment looked up.
translations.idiomatic: It added that former Superintendent Xie Wannian was a flattering traitor who piled taxes and corvée, drove conscripts to sudden death, poisoned the people, and earned the empire's hatred.

sentences.zh: 雖蒙赦令，不宜居京師。」

translations.literal: Although receiving amnesty edict, it is not fitting he dwell in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Even under amnesty he must not remain at the capital.

sentences.zh: 於是湯與萬年俱徙敦煌。

translations.literal: Thereupon Tang and Wannian were both banished to Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang and Xie Wannian were both sent to Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 久之，敦煌太守奏：「湯前親誅郅支單于，威行外國，不宜近邊塞。」

translations.literal: After a long time the Dunhuang grand administrator memorialized: Tang formerly personally executed Chanyu Zhizhi; his awe runs among foreign states; it is not fitting he be near the border passes.
translations.idiomatic: After a time the grand administrator of Dunhuang reported that Chen Tang, who had slain Zhizhi with his own hand, was too fearsome a name to keep on the frontier.

sentences.zh: 詔徙安定。

translations.literal: An edict banished him to Anding.
translations.idiomatic: An edict moved him to Anding.

sentences.zh: 議郎耿育上書言便宜，因冤訟湯曰；

translations.literal: Gentleman consultant Geng Yu submitted a memorial speaking conveniences, thereby wrongly pleading for Tang, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Consultant Geng Yu offered policy advice and pleaded Chen Tang's injustice in these words:

sentences.zh: 「延壽、湯為聖漢揚鉤深致遠之威，雪國家累年之恥，討絕域不羈之君，系萬里難制之虜，豈有比哉！

translations.literal: Yanshou and Tang for the sage Han raised the awe of hooking deep and reaching far, wiped the state's years-long shame, punished the unruly lord of extreme regions, bound the hard-to-control captive beyond ten thousand li—how can there be comparison!
translations.idiomatic: Yanshou and Tang carried the mighty Han's prestige into the deepest west, wiped away years of shame, struck down a lawless chieftain beyond the maps, and bound a foe no nearer garrison could catch—what deed can compare?

sentences.zh: 先帝嘉之，仍下明詔，宣著其功，改年垂歷，傳之無窮。

translations.literal: The former emperor praised them, then issued a clear edict, proclaiming and making manifest their merit, changing the era name and hanging the calendar, transmitting it without end.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor praised them, promulgated an edict that broadcast their deed, changed the era name, and set their fame in the calendar for ages to come.

sentences.zh: 應是，南郡獻白虎，邊陲無警備。

translations.literal: Responding to this, Nan commandery presented a white tiger; the border marches had no alert garrisons.
translations.idiomatic: At that Nan commandery offered a white tiger as tribute and the frontier stood unarmed.

sentences.zh: 會先帝寢疾，然猶垂意不忘，數使尚書責問丞相，趣立其功。

translations.literal: It happened the former emperor lay ill in bed, yet still hung his mind without forgetting, repeatedly sent the secretary to reprove the chancellor, urging establishment of their merit.
translations.idiomatic: Even on his sickbed the late emperor did not forget, and repeatedly had the secretariat press the chancellor to settle their reward.

sentences.zh: 獨丞相匡衡排而不予，封延壽、湯數百戶，此功臣戰士所以失望也。

translations.literal: Only Chancellor Kuang Heng obstructed and would not grant it, enfeoffing Yanshou and Tang only several hundred households—this is why meritorious ministers and fighting soldiers were disappointed.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Kuang Heng alone blocked full honors and gave Yanshou and Chen Tang only a few hundred households—small wonder the veterans felt betrayed.

sentences.zh: 孝成皇帝承建業之基，乘征伐之威，兵革不動，國家無事，而大臣傾邪，讒佞在朝，曾不深惟本末之難，以防未然之戒，欲專主威，排妒有功，使湯塊然被冤拘囚，不能自明，卒以無罪，老棄敦煌，正當西域通道，令威名折衝之臣旅踵及身，復為郅支遺虜所笑，誠可悲也！

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaocheng inherited the foundation of established enterprise, rode the awe of campaigns and attacks; arms and leather did not move, state and family had no affairs, yet great ministers were crooked, slanderers and flatterers were at court; they never deeply pondered root-and-branch difficulty, to guard against not-yet-happened warning; wishing to monopolize the ruler's awe, expelled and envied those with merit, causing Tang lumpishly to bear false charge and be imprisoned, unable to clear himself, in the end by no crime, old abandoned at Dunhuang, right on the Western Regions thoroughfare, making awe-and-name shock-repelling ministers' travel follow on his person, again to be laughed at by Zhizhi's surviving captives—truly pitiable!
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Cheng inherited a peaceful realm yet let crooked ministers and slanderers crowd the court; they never weighed root or branch, never guarded against trouble before it came, but jealously hoarded imperial favor and struck at the meritorious—so Chen Tang sat in chains unable to clear his name, was cleared of crime yet cast off to old age at Dunhuang on the very road to the west, where the very name that once awed the nomads now draws their laughter. It is a bitter shame.

sentences.zh: 至今奉使外蠻者，未嘗不陳郅支之誅以揚漢國之盛。

translations.literal: Down to today those who carry mission to outer barbarians never fail to cite Zhizhi's execution in order to raise the Han state's splendor.
translations.idiomatic: Every envoy to the tribes still cites Zhizhi's execution to magnify Han glory.

sentences.zh: 夫援人之功以懼敵，棄人之身以快讒，豈不痛哉！

translations.literal: To borrow another man's merit to awe the enemy, yet cast aside his person to gratify slanderers—is this not painful!
translations.idiomatic: To lean on a man's victory to overawe the foe and then cast his body aside to please the envious—how can that not wound the heart!

sentences.zh: 且安不忘危，盛必慮衰，今國家素無文帝累年節儉富饒之畜，又無武帝薦延梟俊禽敵之臣，獨有一陳湯耳！

translations.literal: Moreover in security one does not forget danger; in splendor one must consider decline. Now the state has never had the stores of frugal plenty amassed over many years under Emperor Wen, nor has it the fierce and able ministers whom Emperor Wu promoted to seize the foe—there is only Chen Tang!
translations.idiomatic: In peace we must remember peril, and at the height of power we must look to decline. The realm has never piled up the frugal surpluses of Emperor Wen's long reign, nor does it field the fierce talent Emperor Wu raised to strike the enemy—there is only Chen Tang left!

sentences.zh: 假使異世不及陛下，尚望國家追錄其功，封表其墓，以勸後進也。

translations.literal: Suppose in another generation he had not reached your majesty's time; we would still hope the state would pursue and record his merit, enfeoff or mark his tomb, so as to encourage those who come after.
translations.idiomatic: Had he lived in another age and never reached your throne, we would still expect the court to remember his deed, honor his tomb, and so hearten those who follow.

sentences.zh: 湯幸得身當聖世，功曾未久，反聽邪臣鞭逐斥遠，使亡逃分竄，死無處所。

translations.literal: Tang was fortunate enough to have his person meet this sage age; his merit had not yet long rested, yet he instead listened to wicked ministers who whipped and drove him away in reproach, causing him to flee and scatter separately, with nowhere to die.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang was lucky enough to serve a sage reign, yet before his deed had even cooled, corrupt ministers had him whipped into exile, driven to scatter in flight with nowhere to lay his bones.

sentences.zh: 遠覽之士，莫不計度，以為湯功累世不可及，而湯過人情所有，湯尚如此，雖復破絕筋骨，暴露形骸，猶復製於脣舌，為嫉妒之臣所繫虜耳。

translations.literal: Men of far-ranging view all calculated and reckoned that Tang's merit successive ages could not match, yet Tang's faults were what human feeling possesses; if even Tang was thus, then though one again splinter bones and sinews and expose form and frame to the elements, one would still be controlled by lips and tongue and be bound captive by jealous ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Thoughtful men everywhere have weighed it and judged Chen Tang's feat beyond any age to match, while his faults were only what any man might show; if even he was treated like this, then though you shatter sinews and bare your body to wind and sun, jealous tongues will still hold you prisoner.

sentences.zh: 此臣所以為國家尤戚戚也。」

translations.literal: This is why your servant is especially sorrowful for the state."
translations.idiomatic: That is why your servant grieves all the more for the realm."

sentences.zh: 書奏，天子還湯，卒於長安。

translations.literal: When the memorial was presented, the Son of Heaven recalled Tang; he died in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: When the memorial reached the throne, the emperor recalled Chen Tang, who ended his days in Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 死後數年，王莽為安漢公秉政，既內德湯舊恩，又欲諂皇太后，以討郅支功尊元帝廟稱高宗。

translations.literal: Several years after his death, Wang Mang as Duke Who Pacifies the Han wielded government; inwardly he valued Tang's old kindness, and also wished to flatter the Empress Dowager; by the merit of punishing Zhizhi he honored Emperor Yuan's temple with the title Gaozong.
translations.idiomatic: Some years after Chen Tang died, Wang Mang took power as Duke Who Pacifies Han; he remembered Tang's old kindness, wished to gratify the Empress Dowager, and cited the Zhizhi campaign to have Emperor Yuan's temple honored as Gaozong.

sentences.zh: 以湯、延壽前功大賞薄，及候丞杜勳不賞，乃益封延壽孫遷千六百戶，追謚湯曰破胡壯侯，封湯子馮為破胡侯，勳為討狄侯。

translations.literal: Because Tang and Yanshou's earlier merit was great but reward thin, and Assistant for the Marquis Du Xun was not rewarded, he then added enfeoffment to Yanshou's grandson Qian of 1,600 households, posthumously titled Tang as Marquis Zhuang who Broke the Hu, enfeoffed Tang's son Feng as Marquis who Broke the Hu, and Xun as Marquis who Punished the Di.
translations.idiomatic: Judging Tang and Gan Yanshou's deed too great for the meager honors they had received, and noting that Assistant for the Marquis Du Xun had gone unrewarded, he added 1,600 households to Yanshou's grandson Qian, posthumously titled Tang as Marquis Zhuang who Broke the Hu, made Tang's son Feng Marquis who Broke the Hu, and Xun Marquis who Punished the Di.

sentences.zh: 段會宗

translations.literal: Duan Huizong.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Huizong

sentences.zh: 段會宗字子孫，天水上邽人也。

translations.literal: Duan Huizong, courtesy name Zisun, was a man of Shanggui in Tianshui.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Huizong, courtesy name Zisun, came from Shanggui in Tianshui commandery.

sentences.zh: 竟寧中，以杜陵令五府舉為西域都護、騎都尉、光祿大夫。

translations.literal: In the Jingning period, from his post as magistrate of Duling he was recommended by the five offices and made Protector-General of the Western Regions, Commandant of Cavalry, and Imperial Household Grandee.
translations.idiomatic: During Jingning, on nomination from the five offices while he was magistrate of Duling, he was appointed Protector-General of the Western Regions, Commandant of Cavalry, and Imperial Household Grandee.

sentences.zh: 西域敬其威信。

translations.literal: The Western Regions respected his authority and trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: The Western Regions stood in awe of his authority and good faith.

sentences.zh: 三歲，更盡還，拜為沛郡太守。

translations.literal: Three years, his tour of duty ended and he returned, being appointed Grand Administrator of Pei commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When his three-year term was up he came home and was named Grand Administrator of Pei.

sentences.zh: 以單于當朝，徙為雁門太守。

translations.literal: Because the Chanyu was attending court, he was transferred to be Grand Administrator of Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Chanyu was at court he was shifted to Grand Administrator of Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 數年，坐法免。

translations.literal: After several years, he sat for an offense under the law and was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: A few years later he lost his post for a legal infraction.

sentences.zh: 西域諸國上書願得會宗，陽朔中復為都護。

translations.literal: The various states of the Western Regions submitted memorials wishing to obtain Huizong; in the Yangshuo period he again became Protector-General.
translations.idiomatic: The Western Regions petitioned to have him back, and in the Yangshuo era he was again made Protector-General.

sentences.zh: 會宗為人好大節，矜功名，與谷永相友善。

translations.literal: Huizong as a person valued great integrity, prided himself on merit and fame, and was mutually friendly with Gu Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Huizong was a man of large principle who prized reputation; he and Gu Yong were close friends.

sentences.zh: 谷永閔其老復遠出，予書戒曰：「足下以柔遠之令德，復典都護之重職，甚休甚休！

translations.literal: Gu Yong pitied him for being old and again going far out, and gave him a letter warning, saying: "You, sir, by virtue that softens the distant, again hold the weighty post of Protector-General—most excellent, most excellent!
translations.idiomatic: Gu Yong pitied his age and the long journey ahead and wrote to caution him: "You bring the virtue that wins over distant peoples and again shoulder the Protector-General's heavy charge—splendid, splendid!

sentences.zh: 若子之材，可優遊都城而取卿相，何必勒功昆山之仄，總領百蠻，懷柔殊俗？

translations.literal: With your talent, you could idle at ease in the capital and take minister or chancellor—why must you inscribe merit on Kun mountain's flank, chiefly lead the hundred barbarians, embrace and soften different customs?
translations.idiomatic: A man of your gifts could idle in the capital and rise to minister or chancellor—why carve your name on the Kunlun slopes, command every tribe, and court alien ways?

sentences.zh: 子之所長，愚無以喻。

translations.literal: What you excel in, my dullness has no means to describe.
translations.idiomatic: Your strengths are more than my dull wit can praise.

sentences.zh: 雖然，朋友以言贈行，敢不略意。

translations.literal: Even so, as friends we use words to gift the traveler—I dare not omit my thoughts.
translations.idiomatic: Still, friends send words with the traveler—I must speak my mind.

sentences.zh: 方今漢德隆盛，遠人賓服，傅、鄭、甘、陳之功沒齒不可復見，願吾子因循舊貫，毋求奇功，終更亟還，亦足以復雁門之踦，萬里之外以身為本。

translations.literal: Now Han virtue is lofty and splendid; distant people guest-submit; the merits of Fu, Zheng, Gan, and Chen to the end of the teeth cannot again be seen. I hope you will follow the old track, not seek strange merit, finish your tour and quickly return—that also is enough to redeem Yanmen's lameness; beyond ten thousand li, take your person as root.
translations.idiomatic: Han power stands at its height and the outer tribes submit; the age of Fu, Zheng, Gan, and Chen will not return in our lifetime. Follow the beaten path, chase no strange glory, finish your term and hurry home—that alone will mend the stumble at Yanmen; beyond the ten-thousand-li marches, guard your own life first.

sentences.zh: 願詳思愚言。」

translations.literal: I hope you will ponder in detail my foolish words."
translations.idiomatic: Think long on these blunt words."

sentences.zh: 會宗既出，諸國遣子弟郊迎。

translations.literal: When Huizong had gone out, the various states sent sons and younger brothers to greet him in the suburbs.
translations.idiomatic: As soon as Huizong set out, the states sent their princes to meet him beyond the walls.

sentences.zh: 小昆彌安日前為會宗所立，德之，欲往謁，諸翕侯止不聽，遂至龜茲謁。

translations.literal: The Lesser Kunmi Angu, who earlier had been established by Huizong, felt gratitude and wished to go pay visit; the various Xihou stopped him and he did not listen, so he reached Kucha to pay visit.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Kunmi Angu, whom Huizong had placed on the throne, wished to call on him in gratitude; the Xihou tried to stop him, but he went anyway and paid his respects at Kucha.

sentences.zh: 城郭甚親附。

translations.literal: The walled states were very intimate in attachment.
translations.idiomatic: The oasis states drew close to him with warm loyalty.

sentences.zh: 康居太子保蘇匿率眾萬餘人欲降，會宗奏狀，漢遣衛司馬逢迎。

translations.literal: The Kangju crown prince Baosuni led a crowd of more than ten thousand wishing to surrender; Huizong memorialized the situation; Han sent the Guards major to meet and welcome.
translations.idiomatic: Kangju's crown prince Baosuni brought over ten thousand followers who wished to submit; Huizong reported it, and the court sent a Guards major to receive them.

sentences.zh: 會宗發戊己校尉兵隨司馬受降。

translations.literal: Huizong mobilized the Wu and Ji colonels' troops to follow the major in accepting surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Huizong called out the Wu and Ji colonels' men to escort the major and take the surrender.

sentences.zh: 司馬畏其眾，欲令降者皆自縛，保蘇匿怨望，舉眾亡去。

translations.literal: The major feared their multitude and wished to make the surrenderers all bind themselves; Baosuni nursed resentment, raised the whole host and fled away.
translations.idiomatic: The major, fearing their numbers, ordered every man to bind his own hands; Baosuni took offense and led the whole party away.

sentences.zh: 會宗更盡還，以擅發戊己校尉之兵乏興，有詔贖論。

translations.literal: When Huizong's tour ended and he returned, for having on his own authority mobilized the Wu and Ji colonels' troops and causing shortage in corvée transport, there was an edict for ransom-discussion.
translations.idiomatic: When his term ended and he returned, he was faulted for calling out the Wu and Ji colonels without orders and straining the supply trains; an edict allowed him to commute the sentence with a fine.

sentences.zh: 拜為金城太守，以病免。

translations.literal: He was appointed Grand Administrator of Jincheng, and by illness was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: He was appointed Grand Administrator of Jincheng but resigned on grounds of illness.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，小昆彌為國民所殺，諸翕侯大亂。

translations.literal: After more than a year, the Lesser Kunmi was killed by the state's people; the various Xihou were greatly in disorder.
translations.idiomatic: A year later the Lesser Kunmi was slain by his own people and the Xihou factions fell into chaos.

sentences.zh: 征會宗為左曹中郎將、光祿大夫，使安輯烏孫，立小昆彌兄末振將，定其國而還。

translations.literal: Huizong was summoned as General of the Household for the Left, Imperial Household Grandee, sent to pacify and gather Wusun, establishing the Lesser Kunmi's elder brother Mozhenjiang, settling their state and returning.
translations.idiomatic: The court summoned Huizong as General of the Household for the Left and Imperial Household Grandee to pacify Wusun, set Mozhenjiang—the Lesser Kunmi's elder brother—on the throne, quiet the realm, and return.

sentences.zh: 明年，末振將殺大昆彌，會病死，漢恨誅不加。

translations.literal: The next year Mozhenjiang killed the Greater Kunmi; he happened to fall ill and die; Han resented that punishment was not applied.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Mozhenjiang murdered the Greater Kunmi, then died of illness before Han could execute him—a bitter frustration for the court.

sentences.zh: 元延中，復遣會宗發戊己校尉諸國兵，即誅末振將太子番丘。

translations.literal: In the Yuanyan period, he again sent Huizong to mobilize the Wu and Ji colonels' and various states' troops, thereupon to execute Mozhenjiang's crown prince Fanqiu.
translations.idiomatic: During Yuanyan the court again ordered Huizong to call out the Wu and Ji colonels and allied troops and put Mozhenjiang's heir Fanqiu to death.

sentences.zh: 會宗恐大兵入烏孫，驚番丘，亡逃不可得，即留所發兵墊婁地，選精兵三十弩，逕至昆彌所在，召番丘，責以：「末振將骨肉相殺，殺漢公主子孫，未伏誅而死，使者受詔誅番丘。」

translations.literal: Huizong feared that if great troops entered Wusun they would alarm Fanqiu so he could not be caught in flight; therefore he left the troops he had mobilized at Dianlou ground, selected crack troops of thirty crossbowmen, went straight to where the Kunmi was, summoned Fanqiu, and reproached him with: "Mozhenjiang by flesh-and-bone killed each other, killed the Han princess's descendants; he did not submit to execution before dying; the envoy received the edict to execute Fanqiu."
translations.idiomatic: Huizong feared a large force would stampede Wusun and let Fanqiu slip away, so he parked the main body at Dianlou, took thirty picked crossbowmen straight to the Kunmi's camp, summoned Fanqiu, and charged him: "Mozhenjiang murdered his own kin and the line of the Han princess; he escaped the axe only by dying. The envoy bears an edict to deal with you, Fanqiu."

sentences.zh: 即手劍擊殺番丘。

translations.literal: Thereupon with his own hand and sword he struck and killed Fanqiu.
translations.idiomatic: He drew his own sword and cut Fanqiu down on the spot.

sentences.zh: 官屬以下驚恐，馳歸。

translations.literal: The officials and subordinates were frightened and alarmed, galloped back.
translations.idiomatic: His staff panicked and fled for their lives.

sentences.zh: 小昆彌烏犁靡者，末振將兄子也，勒兵數千騎圍會宗，會宗為言來誅之意：「今圍守殺我，如取漢牛一毛耳。

translations.literal: The Lesser Kunmi Wulimi was Mozhenjiang's elder brother's son; he gathered troops of several thousand horse and surrounded Huizong; Huizong for him stated the intention of coming to execute: "Now if you surround and guard and kill me, it is like plucking one hair from Han's ox.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Kunmi Wulimi, Mozhenjiang's nephew, ringed Huizong with thousands of horsemen. Huizong told him why he had come: "Surround me and cut me down, and you take no more from Han than a single hair from its ox—

sentences.zh: 宛王郅支頭縣槁街，烏孫所知也。」

translations.literal: Zhizhi's head hung displayed at Gaojie—Wusun knows of this."
translations.idiomatic: Zhizhi's head once hung along Gaojie in Chang'an, and Wusun has not forgotten."

sentences.zh: 昆彌以下服，曰：「末振將負漢，誅其子可也，獨不可告我，令飲食之邪？」

translations.literal: The Kunmi downward submitted, saying: "Mozhenjiang betrayed Han; to execute his son is acceptable—only could you not have told me, to let me give him drink and food?"
translations.idiomatic: The Kunmi and his nobles yielded and said, "Mozhenjiang wronged Han; his son may die for it—but could you not have warned us and let us give the boy a last meal?"

sentences.zh: 會宗曰：「豫告昆彌，逃匿之，為大罪。

translations.literal: Huizong said: "If I had forewarned the Kunmi, and he hid and fled, that would be a great crime.
translations.idiomatic: Huizong said, "Had I warned you first and you let him slip away in hiding, you would have committed a grave offense.

sentences.zh: 即飲食以付我，傷骨肉恩，故不先告。」

translations.literal: If I then with drink and food handed him over to me, it would wound flesh-and-bone kindness; therefore I did not foretell."
translations.idiomatic: To share a meal with him and then hand him over to me would have cut against the bonds of kin—that is why I gave no warning beforehand."

sentences.zh: 昆彌以下號泣罷去。

translations.literal: The Kunmi downward wailed and wept, then stopped and departed.
translations.idiomatic: The Kunmi and his court wept aloud, then withdrew.

sentences.zh: 會宗還奏事，公卿議會宗權得便宜，以輕兵深入烏孫，即誅番丘。

translations.literal: When Huizong returned and reported affairs, the excellencies discussed that Huizong by authority obtained convenience, with light troops deeply entered Wusun, thereupon executed Fanqiu.
translations.idiomatic: Back at court the high ministers ruled that Huizong had rightly used discretionary power: he took a light column deep into Wusun and killed Fanqiu at once.

sentences.zh: 宣明國威，宜加重賞。

translations.literal: He proclaimed and made clear state awe; he ought receive heavy reward.
translations.idiomatic: He had broadcast Han majesty and deserved a rich reward.

sentences.zh: 天子賜會宗爵關內侯，黃金百斤。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven bestowed on Huizong rank of marquis within the passes, one hundred jin of gold.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor ennobled him as marquis within the passes and gave him a hundred jin of gold.

sentences.zh: 是時，小昆彌季父卑爰□擁眾欲害昆彌，漢復遣會宗使安輯，與都護孫建並力。

translations.literal: At this time the Lesser Kunmi's paternal uncle Beiaizhi (text damaged) gathered a host wishing to harm the Kunmi; Han again sent Huizong to pacify and gather, joining forces with Protector-General Sun Jian.
translations.idiomatic: Then the Lesser Kunmi's uncle Be'ai Zhi (character missing in received text) raised troops to strike the Kunmi, and Han again sent Huizong to restore order, working alongside Protector-General Sun Jian.

sentences.zh: 明年，會宗病死烏孫中，年七十五矣，城郭諸國為發喪立祠焉。

translations.literal: The next year Huizong by illness died within Wusun, aged seventy-five; the walled states for him conducted mourning and established shrines.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he died of illness in Wusun at the age of seventy-five, and the oasis states mourned him and raised shrines in his honor.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：自元狩之際，張騫始通西域，至於地節，鄭吉建都護之號，訖王莽世，凡十八人，皆以勇略選，然其有功跡者具此。

translations.literal: The appraiser says: From the time of Yuanshou, Zhang Qian first opened the Western Regions; down to Dijie, Zheng Ji established the title Protector-General; down to Wang Mang's age, in all eighteen men, all were selected by courage and strategy—yet those who had meritorious traces are fully set forth here.
translations.idiomatic: The historian's judgment: From Zhang Qian's opening of the west in Yuanshou to Zheng Ji's creation of the Protector-General's office in Dijie, eighteen men held the post through Wang Mang's day, each chosen for courage and stratagem; those who left a clear record are gathered in this chapter.

sentences.zh: 廉褒以恩信稱，郭舜以廉平著，孫建用威重顯，其餘無稱焉。

translations.literal: Lian Bao was praised for kindness and trustworthiness; Guo Shun was noted for incorruptibility and fairness; Sun Jian was manifest through awesome weight; the rest had no praise.
translations.idiomatic: Lian Bao won repute for kindness and good faith, Guo Shun for honest even-handed rule, Sun Jian for stern authority; the others left little mark.

sentences.zh: 陳湯儻□，不自收斂，卒用困窮，議者閔之，故備列云。

translations.literal: Chen Tang recklessly (text damaged), did not himself gather in restraint, in the end thereby used poverty and distress; discussers pitied him; therefore they are fully arrayed here.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Tang was rash and unbridled (one character missing in the text), never learned restraint, and ended in want and exile; later writers pitied him, so his whole story is set out here.
